START.label: End of Day Reflection
START.message: Hey. Today happened. Before you close the laptop — let's spend 3 minutes making sense of it. No right answers here.

Q1_LOCUS_ENERGY.label: Where did your energy go today?
Q1_LOCUS_ENERGY.message: When you think back on today — where did most of your mental energy actually go?

options.text: Dealing with things that kept getting in my way
Figuring out how to move something forward despite resistance
Waiting for clarity or direction that didn't come
Choosing what to focus on and why

D1_LOCUS_ENERGY.label: Route based on Locus signal from Q1

Q2_LOCUS_CREDIT.label: When something went sideways today...
Q2_LOCUS_CREDIT.message: Think of one thing that didn't go as planned today. What's your honest first read on it?

options.text: Someone or something caused it — I could see it coming
options.psychology: Blame attribution — causality placed on environment

options.text: I probably could have handled a part of it differently
It was bad luck or bad timing — not really about anyone
I made a call that made sense then, but I'd refine it now

D2_LOCUS_CREDIT.label: Accumulate Locus signal — route to final Locus question

Q3_LOCUS_SCENARIO_VICTIM.label: The meeting that went nowhere
Q3_LOCUS_SCENARIO_VICTIM.message: A meeting ran long and blocked your afternoon. You left frustrated, behind on work. What do you tell yourself tonight?

options.text: The organizer wasted everyone's time — it shouldn't have happened
options.psychology: Externalizing — assigns full responsibility to others

options.text: I could have flagged early that the meeting was going off-track
options.psychology: Internal agency — recognizes influence within system

options.text: These things happen in teams — I'll recover tomorrow
I should have blocked that time — I can protect it better

Q3_LOCUS_SCENARIO_VICTOR.label: The moment you had a choice
Q3_LOCUS_SCENARIO_VICTOR.message: Think of a moment today where the situation was messy but you had a real choice in how to respond. What did you do?

options.text: I waited to see what others would do first
I made a call and stood by it, even without full info
I tried something, watched how it landed, and adjusted
I flagged the ambiguity and asked someone else to decide
options.psychology: Partial external — upward delegation of agency

D3_LOCUS_FINAL.label: Final Locus classification — route to appropriate reflection

REFLECT_LOCUS_EXTERNAL.label: Reflection: External Locus
REFLECT_LOCUS_EXTERNAL.message: It sounds like today felt a lot like things were happening *to* you. That's a real feeling — and sometimes it's accurate. But here's a small thing worth sitting with: even in the moments where you had no control over what happened, was there a moment — even tiny — where you chose how to respond? Not asking you to rewrite the day. Just notice if that moment existed.

REFLECT_LOCUS_INTERNAL.label: Reflection: Internal Locus
REFLECT_LOCUS_INTERNAL.message: You seem to have held the reins pretty firmly today — owning what happened, making calls, not waiting for rescue. One thing worth checking: did you give yourself credit for that? Or did it just feel like 'what you were supposed to do'? The people who stay grounded long-term are the ones who notice when they showed up.

REFLECT_LOCUS_MIXED.label: Reflection: Mixed Locus
REFLECT_LOCUS_MIXED.message: Interesting — today seems like it had both. Moments you owned, moments that felt out of reach. That's actually more honest than most people admit. The question isn't whether that split happened. It's which one you want to feed going forward.

BRIDGE_AXIS1_TO_AXIS2.label: Transition: Locus → Orientation
BRIDGE_AXIS1_TO_AXIS2.message: You just thought about *where* your agency lived today. Now let's look at *what* you were aiming at — were you focused on what you deserved, or what you could give?

Q4_ORIENT_EXPECTATION.label: What were you tracking most today?
Q4_ORIENT_EXPECTATION.message: As the day unfolded, what were you most mentally tracking?

options.text: Whether I was getting the recognition or support I needed
Whether the work I was doing was actually useful to someone
Whether I was being given fair opportunities compared to others
Whether I was working on what mattered most right now

D4_ORIENT_EXPECT.label: Route based on Orientation signal from Q4

Q5_ORIENT_COLLAB.label: When a colleague needed something from you...
Q5_ORIENT_COLLAB.message: Imagine — or recall — a colleague asked for your input today on something that wasn't really 'your job.' What was your internal reaction?

options.text: Mild frustration — I'm already stretched, this isn't my problem
options.psychology: Scarcity + boundary as entitlement — zero-sum framing

options.text: Genuine interest — I like knowing what's happening across the team
Calculation — will this get noticed, does it benefit me?
options.psychology: Transactional entitlement — help contingent on return

options.text: Quick check on capacity, then engagement — if I can, I will

Q6_ORIENT_VALUE.label: End of day honest check
Q6_ORIENT_VALUE.message: If your team had to describe today's version of you — not the ideal you, today's actual you — what would ring most true?

options.text: They'd say I was frustrated and hard to reach
options.psychology: Withdrawal signal — entitlement expressed as unavailability

options.text: They'd say I pushed things forward and made decisions
They'd say I asked for a lot but didn't give much back
They'd say I solved one real problem for someone

REFLECT_ORIENT_ENTITLEMENT.label: Reflection: Entitlement Orientation
REFLECT_ORIENT_ENTITLEMENT.message: There's a version of today where you were keeping score — tracking what came in vs. what went out. That's not wrong, it's human. But here's what's worth noticing: the people you probably trust most at work aren't the ones thinking about what they deserve. They're the ones where you can feel they're genuinely trying to make something work. Was there a moment today where you felt that way — even briefly?

REFLECT_ORIENT_CONTRIBUTION.label: Reflection: Contribution Orientation
REFLECT_ORIENT_CONTRIBUTION.message: Sounds like today, you were mostly trying to move things forward — for the work, for others. That's a real thing and it's worth naming. One small check though: were you contributing *from* energy or *despite* empty? Sustainable contribution has a floor. Did you protect yours today?

REFLECT_ORIENT_MIXED.label: Reflection: Mixed Orientation
REFLECT_ORIENT_MIXED.message: Today seems like it pulled you in both directions — moments of genuine giving, and moments of tallying up. Both are real. The question that might be worth sleeping on: which orientation left you feeling better at the end of the day?

BRIDGE_AXIS2_TO_AXIS3.label: Transition: Orientation → Radius
BRIDGE_AXIS2_TO_AXIS3.message: You looked at what drove you. Now let's zoom out one more ring — were you thinking mostly about yourself today, or were others in your frame?

Q7_RADIUS_AWARENESS.label: Who was in your mind today?
Q7_RADIUS_AWARENESS.message: As decisions got made today — big and small — whose perspective was actually in the room with you mentally?

options.text: Mostly mine — I was focused on getting my work done
My immediate team — I thought about how my work affects them
Honestly — just the people I needed something from
options.psychology: Instrumental others — others as means, not ends

options.text: The people downstream — users, stakeholders, whoever this lands on

D7_RADIUS_AWARE.label: Route based on Radius signal from Q7

Q8_RADIUS_IMPACT.label: Did you check in on anyone today?
Q8_RADIUS_IMPACT.message: Not on Slack tasks — but genuinely check in. Did you notice how someone was doing today?

options.text: No — I was too heads-down to look up
Yes — I noticed someone struggling and said something
I noticed but didn't act on it — felt like not my place
options.psychology: Bystander inhibition — awareness without radius extension

options.text: Someone checked on me — I hadn't thought to return it

Q9_RADIUS_FINAL.label: Tomorrow — who are you thinking about?
Q9_RADIUS_FINAL.message: When you think about tomorrow's work, who enters your mind first?

options.text: My own task list and what I need to finish
A specific person I want to help move forward
The outcome we're all trying to get to together
What I didn't get done and need to protect tomorrow

D9_RADIUS_FINAL.label: Final Radius classification + build summary signals

REFLECT_RADIUS_SELF.label: Reflection: Self Radius
REFLECT_RADIUS_SELF.message: Today seems like you were mostly in your own lane — not in a bad way, but the radius was tight. That's sometimes exactly what a day needs. But here's a thread worth pulling: when was the last time someone at work left an interaction with you feeling a little clearer, a little lighter? Not your job to manage their emotions — just noticing where your presence lands.

REFLECT_RADIUS_OTHERS.label: Reflection: Others Radius
REFLECT_RADIUS_OTHERS.message: You seem to have kept other people genuinely in view today — which is rarer than it sounds. Expanding your radius takes something. The check worth doing: were you doing it from real interest, or from a sense that you *should* be thinking about others? Both produce the same behavior, but they feel very different from the inside.

REFLECT_RADIUS_MIXED.label: Reflection: Mixed Radius
REFLECT_RADIUS_MIXED.message: Your radius today seemed to shift depending on the situation — which is actually pretty sophisticated. The people who grow fastest aren't the ones who are always other-focused. They're the ones who notice when their radius is contracting and ask themselves why.

SUMMARY_NODE.label: Full Day Reflection Summary

axis1_summary.dominant_internal: You mostly held agency — owned what happened.
axis1_summary.dominant_external: Things felt like they were happening to you more than through you.
axis1_summary.mixed: A split day — owned some, externalized some.

axis2_summary.dominant_contribution: You were tracking impact over entitlement.
axis2_summary.dominant_entitlement: You were tracking fairness and return more than contribution.

axis3_summary.dominant_others: Others were genuinely in your frame.
axis3_summary.dominant_self: You were mostly in your own lane today.
axis3_summary.mixed: Your radius expanded and contracted situationally.

END.label: Close
END.message:
That's it. Close the laptop. You showed up today. That counts.

— DeepThought Reflection System

path.text: Dealing with things that kept getting in my way
Someone or something caused it — I could see it coming
The organizer wasted everyone's time — it shouldn't have happened
Whether I was getting the recognition or support I needed
Mild frustration — I'm already stretched, this isn't my problem
They'd say I asked for a lot but didn't give much back
Mostly mine — I was focused on getting my work done
No — I was too heads-down to look up
My own task list and what I need to finish
Figuring out how to move something forward despite resistance
I made a call that made sense then, but I'd refine it now
I tried something, watched how it landed, and adjusted
Whether the work I was doing was actually useful to someone
Quick check on capacity, then engagement — if I can, I will
They'd say I solved one real problem for someone
The people downstream — users, stakeholders, whoever this lands on
Yes — I noticed someone struggling and said something
The outcome we're all trying to get to together
